title: Data Analyst (m/f/d) - Order-to-Cash
required_skills: Several years of experience in finance environment combined with business intelligence/analytics
Strong expertise in BI and reporting tools (Power BI, SAP SAC, Datasphere)
Good understanding of O2C data structures (credit, invoicing, receivables)
Experience with SAP SAC, SAP Datasphere, or Celonis
matched_skills: Power BI: PL-300 certified — strong match for BI and reporting tools requirement
Data storytelling and analytical reporting: HSBC C-suite dashboards, sprint review reports at Hapag-Lloyd
Stakeholder orientation: cross-functional reporting and presentation experience
missing_skills: Order-to-Cash process expertise (accounts receivable, DSO, credit risk, cash allocation) — no O2C background
SQL skills (no SQL mentioned anywhere in CV)
SAP SAC, SAP Datasphere (no SAP experience)
ERP system experience of any kind
Finance environment analytics experience (HSBC was programme governance, not finance analytics)
core_responsibilities: Design, build, and maintain reporting and analytics for order-to-cash processes
Operate and enhance data pipelines across credit management, accounts receivable, autobank, customer master data
Perform descriptive, diagnostic, and predictive analyses (DSO, credit risk, payment terms)
Create and maintain regular O2C reports and dashboards
Conduct ad-hoc analyses and present insights to management
Develop anomaly detection for invoices, payments, master data changes
recommended_emphasis: Power BI PL-300 certification is a genuine asset — lead with this for the BI tooling requirement
The O2C domain expertise is a hard gap; frame HSBC financial tracking as adjacent but be honest about the domain difference
SQL is explicitly required — the absence of SQL skills is a critical gap that must be disclosed
This role is predominantly a data/analytics specialist role, not a PMO role; the fit is limited
summary: Match score 27.3% — weak match. The candidate's Power BI PL-300 certification directly hits the primary BI tool requirement, but the role requires deep Order-to-Cash domain expertise, SQL proficiency, and SAP analytics experience that the candidate lacks entirely. The O2C specialisation and SQL requirement are hard gaps for a data analyst role. Application is only recommended if the candidate is willing to invest in SQL skills development.
